design_philosophy.core_loop: Character progression through skill trees and faction alignment while completing quests in alternate history 1588 Europe. Solo protagonist with AI party members in isometric turn-based/real-time hybrid combat.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral alignment choices through faction selection (Knights Templar, Inquisition, Knights of Saladin, Wielders) while pursuing the main quest to prevent permanent dimensional rift opening.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing a descendant of Richard the Lionheart inheriting supernatural powers in a magical alternate history, interacting with Renaissance historical figures (da Vinci, Shakespeare, Galileo) reimagined in a supernatural setting.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to leveraging SPECIAL system from Fallout franchise for character building depth; alternate history premise that recontextualizes medieval crusades and Renaissance era through supernatural lens; faction-based narrative branches that force ideological choices.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character creation with traits system allows player-defined difficulty modulation through stat allocation and trait selection. Skill diversity (diplomacy, sneak, combat magic) enables multiple approach strategies to encounters.

progression.upgrade_system: SPECIAL system with character stat allocation (Strength, Perception, Endurance, Charisma, Intelligence, Agility, Luck). Skill trees for abilities including combat, diplomacy, sneak, and magical skills. Perk system grants bonuses during game progression (e.g., Superior Senses). Spiritkind selection (demonic, elemental, bestial) provides persistent character personality and occasional narrative guidance.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Faction alignment unlocks different questlines and story branches. Skills unlock through point distribution and leveling. Perks unlock through progression milestones.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct faction paths (Knights Templar, Inquisition, Knights of Saladin, Wielders)
Multiple Spiritkind options affecting narrative tone and character personality
Diverse character builds enabled by skill tree flexibility
Diplomacy and stealth alternatives to combat encounters

world_building_techniques: Alternate history premise: Disjunction event where Richard the Lionheart's massacre at Siege of Acre triggered magical rift into world
Anachronistic historical figures recontextualized: Leonardo da Vinci, Galileo Galilei, Shakespeare, Cervantes, Machiavelli appear as NPCs in 1588 alternate Barcelona
Spiritkind narration device: player's spirit companion explains mechanics and advances plot through character voice
Faction NPC interactions providing ideological worldbuilding (Inquisition rampant, Spanish Armada threat, dimensional rift destabilization)
1588 Renaissance setting with supernatural overlay creating genre-blend atmosphere

danger_signals: Dimensional rift threat to reality itself
Demons and supernatural beings entering mortal realm
Spanish Armada invasion threat to England
environmental_storytelling: Alternate history Barcelona under Inquisition pressure
Renaissance cultural figures displaced into magical world
Medieval crusade history reframed through supernatural lens
Monster-infested areas as consequence of Disjunction event
